blocks.text: Inspect for blown No. C25 (20A) fuse in the fuse box and connections before performing the inspection.
1. Wrap aluminum foil (B) around the end of the tester rod (A) as shown in the figure.

items.text: If there is no battery voltage, inspect the following items
Auto A/C control unit input inspection
Cord disconnection and terminal connection condition
If battery voltage is present, step 4 Proceed to

blocks.text: 4. Connect the positive terminal of the voltmeter to the center of each heat wire (A) and the negative terminal to (-), and confirm that there is approximately 6 V.

items.text: If the voltage is at the specified value, it is normal.
If the voltage shows a value other than the specified value, repair the hot wire.
If battery voltage is present, there is a disconnection on the (-) terminal side from the center.
If battery voltage is present, there is a disconnection on the (-) terminal side from the center.
If there is no voltage, there is a disconnection on the (+) terminal side from the center.
